page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with an Intel Xeon CPU operating at 2.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This instance features 12 virtual CPUs (6 physical cores with 2 threads per core) and 85.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.08 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU equipped with 40 GB of VRAM. The instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear division between CPU and GPU capabilities. Single-core CPU performance is weak, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, which fall into the bottom 25% category. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations on Redis perform at average levels. Conversely, GPU-driven workloads show top-tier performance, with LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation on small (135M) and medium (7B) models ranking in the top 10%. However, prompt processing for large (70B) models drops to average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a specialized entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads by pairing a high-end NVIDIA A100 GPU with a relatively modest 12-vCPU host configuration. This resource density avoids the cost of over-provisioning CPU resources when the primary bottleneck is GPU compute. The instance is best suited for machine learning inference, medium-scale LLM execution, and GPU-accelerated data science tasks, though it is constrained by the lack of local storage and lower single-threaded CPU performance.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon x86_64 CPU with 12 vCPUs, 85.0 GB of RAM, and a single NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 40 GB of VRAM. It includes no local storage. Benchmarks reveal weak single-core CPU performance but top-tier results for small and medium LLM inference tasks, which rank in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks remain average. This configuration provides a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-heavy workloads by avoiding excessive CPU allocation. It is designed for machine learning inference, medium-scale language model execution, and GPU-accelerated data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g features 12 vCPUs, 85GB RAM, and one NVIDIA A100 GPU with 40GB VRAM. It delivers top-tier LLM inference speeds despite weak single-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server with 12 vCPUs, 85GB RAM, and one NVIDIA A100 GPU for machine learning workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized server pairing a single NVIDIA A100 GPU with moderate CPU resources for efficient machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU featuring 40 GB of VRAM
Configured with 12 vCPUs, 85.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage
Top-tier LLM inference performance for small and medium models
Weak single-core CPU performance offset by dedicated GPU acceleration
Suited for machine learning inference and GPU-accelerated data science
